title: bridge-slack: HMAC computed over empty string when body is non-UTF-8
description: bridge-slack/src/slack.rs:102: `std::str::from_utf8(body).unwrap_or("")` silently replaces non-UTF-8 body with an empty string before appending to the HMAC base string. Consequence: if Slack ever sends a request body containing non-UTF-8 bytes (valid JSON can be UTF-8 but intermediaries can corrupt), HMAC is computed over `v0:{ts}:` instead of `v0:{ts}:{body}`, causing legitimate Slack events to fail verification. A forged request with non-UTF-8 body only needs a HMAC of `v0:{ts}:`, requiring signing_secret knowledge — but still violates the contract. Fix: use `from_utf8(body).map_err(|_| anyhow!("non-UTF-8 body"))?` and reject, or compute HMAC over raw bytes without UTF-8 conversion.
